The upcoming review of the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA), set for July 1, has emerged as a political and economic test of the trilateral relationship of North America’s largest economies. Originally conceived as a checkpoint to assess the effectiveness of the trade agreement six years after its implementation, the impending review is taking place during […]

expert_rss.summary:
On March 19, UK law enforcement broke into a hotel room in West Yorkshire to arrest a 19-year-old Norwegian national, Johannes Natland, planning to assassinate a UK-based target. Police announced that Natland was working on behalf of the Iran-aligned criminal organization, Foxtrot. The Swedish criminal network had contacted the teenager through a social media platform […]

expert_rss.title: Innovation in AI-Enabled Harms: The Transference of AI Knowledge from Cybercriminals to Extremists
expert_rss.summary: Violent extremist actors, including Salafi-Jihadists, neo-Nazis, nihilistic violent extremists (NVE), and others, are increasingly adopting artificial intelligence (AI) for novel purposes, including translation, propaganda generation, attack planning, and the spread of misinformation. expert_rss.title: Deep Analysis: The Islamic State is Using the Bondi Attack to Inspire Violence in the West
expert_rss.summary: Since December 2025, the Islamic State (IS), its Khurasan affiliate (ISKP), and aligned pro-IS media outlets have been waging a coordinated propaganda campaign around the Bondi Beach attack in Sydney, in which a father and son opened fire on a Jewish Hanukkah gathering, killing and wounding approximately 15 people.

expert_rss.title: Sarayah Ansar al-Sunnah: Sectarian Violence, Micro-Insurgency, and the Islamic State’s Shadow in Syria
expert_rss.summary: In the spaces left uncovered by the collapse of the Syrian state order and the post-Assad political transition, jihadist violence has not disappeared: it has fragmented, adapted, and sectarianized.

expert_rss.title: War Department Leaders Observe Kansas City's Counter-Drone Preparations Ahead of World Cup
expert_rss.summary: Joint Interagency Task Force 401 continues to share knowledge and best practices with World Cup host cities for countering illicit unmanned aircraft systems.

expert_rss.title: 104th Fighter Wing Strengthens Regional Medical Readiness With Multiday Emergency Decontamination Course
expert_rss.summary: The Massachusetts National Guard's 104th Fighter Wing Medical Group enhanced regional medical readiness by hosting the Air Combat Command Emergency Decontamination Course at Barnes Air National Guard Base, the first time the Air National Guard has hosted a remote training team for the course.

expert_rss.title: Navy Establishes NSA Stirling in Australia
expert_rss.summary: The Navy established Naval Support Activity Stirling in Perth, Australia, to provide essential services and community infrastructure for American personnel and families stationed there as part of the trilateral AUKUS security partnership.

expert_rss.title: AH-64 Apache Shot Down By Iran, U.S. Will Retaliate: Trump (Updated)

expert_rss.title: US launches new strikes on Iran after helicopter downed
expert_rss.summary: The U.S. launched strikes against Iran on Tuesday after Trump said Tehran had shot down a U.S. Apache helicopter in the Strait of Hormuz.

expert_rss.title: Sailor sentenced to 44 years in prison for murder of Angelina Resendiz
expert_rss.summary: Petty Officer Jermiah Copeland strangled Petty Officer Angelina Resendiz to death after a disagreement at Naval Station Norfolk, Virginia.

expert_rss.title: Senator warns of government shutdown over dispute on fiscal 2027 defense budget
expert_rss.summary: Sen. John Kennedy, R-La., said that leaders should prepare for a government shutdown as lawmakers disagree on a defense budget increase.

expert_rss.title: Marine Corps looks to equip armored vehicles with 360-degree cameras
expert_rss.summary: The goal is to allow crews to operate their vehicles – and enjoy full situational awareness – even while buttoned up with the hatches closed.

expert_rss.title: Marines to have their own barracks bedrooms at Japan base
expert_rss.summary: Marines stationed at Camp Hansen, Japan, will have new barracks with individual bedrooms.

expert_rss.title: ‘Flashpoint Campaigns: Cold War’ is the ultimate OODA Loop wargame
expert_rss.summary: Flashpoint Campaigns is an OODA Loop-anchored computer wargame that depicts a hypothetical Warsaw Pact invasion of West Germany in 1989.

expert_rss.title: Army mortar crews trade laptops for phone app
expert_rss.summary: Army mortar teams have replaced 20-year-old software used to calculate firing data and ballistics with a new application designed for smartphones.

expert_rss.title: Pentagon reveals preferred munitions for one-way attack drones
expert_rss.summary: The list includes Northrop Grumman and several startups competing to supply low-cost payloads compatible with any drone design.

expert_rss.title: US soldiers rescued by drone after Apache helicopter goes down near the coast of Oman
expert_rss.summary: U.S. Naval Forces Central Command's Task Force 59 and the Army's 82nd Airborne Division led the rescue, with Air Force assets assisting, as well.

expert_rss.title: Chokepoint busters: Marines seek toolkit to help aviators clear way for amphibs
expert_rss.summary: The Marine Corps wants to learn how airpower might ensure passage through "critical chokepoints" while under threat in a new age of warfare.

expert_rss.title: Dusting a Dirt Road: How The United States Can Break the Cycle of Failing Military Infrastructure
expert_rss.summary: Winter Storm Uri ripped through Texas in January 2021. The frigid temperatures froze pipes, which then burst and caused flooding in aging barracks at Fort Hood, many of which were overdue for renovations and had vulnerable mechanical and utility systems.
